title: Bandhavgarh National Park
tagline: Bandhavgarh National Park — wildlife reserve of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Bandhavgarh National Park is a national park of India, located in the Umaria district of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Bandhavgarh National Park is a national park of India, located in the Umaria district of Madhya Pradesh. It spreads over an area of 105 km2 (41 sq mi) and was declared a national park in 1968. It became Tiger Reserve in 1993 with a core area of 716 km2 (276 sq mi). Bandhavgarh was part of the princely state of Rewa, and it was a hunting destination for kings.

topPlaces.name: Panpatha Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Panpatha is a wildlife sanctuary located in Umaria district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It was established in 1983 and covers approximately 245.84 km² of dry deciduous forest adjoining Bandhavgarh Tiger Reserve.

items.note: Panpatha is a wildlife sanctuary located in Umaria district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It was established in 1983 and covers approximately 245.84 km² of dry deciduous forest adjoining Bandhavgarh Tiger Reserve.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bandhavgarh National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bandhavgarh National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bandhavgarh National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Katni Junction (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bandhavgarh National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bandhavgarh National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bandhavgarh National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Bandhavgarh National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bandhavgarh National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Panpatha Wildlife Sanctuary.
